headline: Deepfake Scams Cost Malaysians RM280 Million in 2025
context: PDRM cybercrime unit attributed RM280 million in losses to deepfake-enabled scams in 2025 a category barely existing in 2022.

cards.big: Deepfake scams cost Malaysians RM280 million in 2025 a new category
cards.sub: Video and audio manipulation enables sophisticated impersonation fraud

cards.big: 850 cases involved deepfake video calls impersonating family or bosses
cards.sub: Average loss RM329,000 far higher than traditional phone scams

cards.big: PDRM has only 12 officers trained in deepfake detection and forensics
cards.sub: Technology for creating convincing deepfakes freely available to anyone

cards.big: Malaysia has no specific law criminalizing malicious deepfake creation
cards.sub: Prosecutors rely on general fraud and CMA not designed for this technology

cards.big: Deepfake crime tests whether legal frameworks can adapt to rapidly evolving manipulation tools
RM280 million deepfake losses. Synthetic media fraud outpaces verification technology and legal frameworks.
